div.div: Chapter . Introduction to the NXTCG ECOMAPP Database

The NextGen Trading Card Game (NXTCG) features exclusive collectible cards inspired by the Rocket Software NextGen Academy and its Rocketeers. The platform empowers you to buy and sell new NXTCG cards. Use this guide to install the NXTCG database, add tables, and troubleshoot common issues you may encounter.
The database design and architecture includes SKU composition, multiplier-based pricing, and normalized entities with referential integrity. NXTCG's database is hosted on Db2 13 for z/OS.
ECOMAPP database is hosted on Db2 for z/OS platform. You can install the ECOMAPP database within TSO/ISPF.
Adding tables to the ECOMAPP Database
In the ECOMAPP database you can add new tables by using TSO/ISPF in the Db2 z/OS platform.
There are common SQL and API error codes when configuring the ECOMAPP database. Solutions are provided for each issue.

div.class: - map/map bookmap/bookmap map bookmap brand or business unit

h1.span: Introduction to the NXTCG ECOMAPP Database

div.class: - topic/div chapter/minitoc chapter/minitoc-bottom div minitoc minitoc-bottom

div.p: The NextGen Trading Card Game (NXTCG) features exclusive collectible cards inspired by the Rocket Software NextGen Academy and its Rocketeers. The platform empowers you to buy and sell new NXTCG cards. Use this guide to install the NXTCG database, add tables, and troubleshoot common issues you may encounter.

ECOMAPP is a Db2 13 for z/OS database for NXTCG. It is compromised of 15 normalized tables with full referential integrity and indexed access paths. Administration is driven by source-controlled DDL and JCL pipelines for build, initialization, statistics, grants, and teardown.

ul.li: Complete SKU-based product catalog (4,824 trading card variants)
16 normalized tables with full referential integrity
Performance indexes on all critical access paths
Split initialization files for mainframe line length compliance
Automated JCL for all database operations

strong.a: Adding tables to the ECOMAPP Database

div.p: The database design and architecture includes SKU composition, multiplier-based pricing, and normalized entities with referential integrity. NXTCG's database is hosted on Db2 13 for z/OS.

ECOMAPP is a normalized, SKU-driven data model with centralized pricing logic and strong referential integrity. The catalog supports 4,824 sellable SKUs derived from 104 base product types and 63 style modifiers, designed to be scalable and consistent with updates.

section.p: The ECOMDB01 database is organized into 15 tablespaces, with each table placed in its own space to support flexibility and management. It includes the USERS, TOKENS, SERIES, STYLES, STYLE_MODIFIERS, PRODUCT_TYPES, PRODUCT_VARIANTS, VARIANT_IMAGES, INVENTORY, CARTS, CART_ITEMS, ADDRESSES, ORDERS, ORDER_ADDRESSES, and ORDER_ITEMS tables, covering the core data structures of the system. Indexing is handled through primary key indexes along with business key indexes to maintain efficient and reliable query performance.

ECOMAPP NXTCG demonstrates a high level of normalization, with the schema aligning with Third Normal Form (3NF). All relations satisfy the requirements of First and Second Normal Forms, and the use of composite‑key tables ensures that each non‑key attribute is fully dependent on the entire primary key.

div.p: ECOMAPP database is hosted on Db2 for z/OS platform. You can install the ECOMAPP database within TSO/ISPF.

section.p: Before installing ECOMAPP, you must have these requirements:

ul.li: Access: You must have access to TSO/ISPF.
Db2: Version 13, subsystem ID <Your SSID>, Db2 software HLQ <Your DB2HLQ>, and a Db2 Connect license.
Storage: 100 tracks for DDL library, 50 tracks for JCL library, 500 MB DASD for tablespaces and indexes.

div.section: Below are steps instructing you on how to install ECOMAPP database within TSO/ISPF.

li.span: Before uploading any files, you must customize the following placeholders throughout all DDL and JCL files:

tr.td: Application user group for DML access

li.span: Create an Initial Partitioned Data Set (PDS) in <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.ALOC. You must create one dataset and upload the ECOMALOC.jcl as a member of that dataset.Your new dataset can be accessed by <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.ALLOC(ECOMALOC). Configure your new dataset with the appropriate quantities, blocks, and record formattings below.

pre.code: Command ===> A Dataset Name: <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.ECOMALOC Management class . . . <blank> Storage class . . . . <blank> Volume serial . . . . <blank> Device type . . . . . <blank or SYSDA> Data class . . . . . . <blank> Space units . . . . . TRKS Primary quantity . . . 5 Secondary quantity . . 2 Directory blocks . . . 2 Record format . . . . FB Record length . . . . 80 Block size . . . . . . 3120 Data set name type . . PDS

li.span: Log into TSO/ISPF and allocate the Initial PDS.
Upload ECOMALOC.jcl via Zowe or by pasting in the code in ISPF. You can find and edit it by using option 3.4 to find <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.ALLOC(ECOMALLOC).
Run ECOMALOC to create DDL and JCL libraries. This job creates the two main PDS libraries for the DDL and JCL files.
Insert option 3.4 in DSLIST.
Navigate to <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.ALLOC(ECOMALOC).
Type SUB next to the ECOMALOC member.
Click Enter.
Upload all SQL DDL files to the <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.DDL library. You can use Zowe to upload members directly from the project repository into the dataset.
Insert option 3.4 in DSLIST.
Navigate to <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.DDL.
Click Enter.
Navigate to the ISPF main menu and insert Option 2.
Insert <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.DDL(<membername>).
Paste in the new member contents.
Upload all JCL job files to the <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.JCL library using TSO/ISPF.
Insert option 3.4 in DSLIST.
Navigate to <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.JCL.
Click Enter.
Navigate to the ISPF main menu and insert Option 2.
Insert <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.DDL(<membername>).
Paste in the new member contents.
Submit the ECOMDDL job to create the database. Submit an ECOMDDL Job in TSO/ISPF.
Insert option 3.4 in DSLIST.
Navigate to <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.JCL.
Insert B for browsing to see the members.
Type SUB next to the ECOMDDL member.
Click Enter
Verify the database creation in TSO/ISPF.
Insert option 2 in SPUFI.
Enter SELECT * FROM SYSIBM.SYSTABLES WHERE CREATOR = '<Your HLQ>';.
li.div: The result will show all of the tables in the database.

li.span: Initialize the Product catalog by submitting an ECPMINIT Job in TSO/ISPF.
Navigate to <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.JCL.
Type SUBnext to ECOMINIT member.
Run statistics for optimal query performance by running ECOMRS.
Navigate to <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.JCL.
Type SUB next to ECOMRS member.
Check the job output in SDSF job status menu.

h2.span: Adding tables to the ECOMAPP Database

div.p: In the ECOMAPP database you can add new tables by using TSO/ISPF in the Db2 z/OS platform.

li.span: Update EO3TABLE.sql
Add CREATE TABLE statement.
Add CREATE UNIQUE INDEX for the primary key.
Add foreign key constraints if applicable.
Update E04INDEX.sql
Add performance indexes for new table in E04INDEX.sql.
Update E05CMNT.sql by adding a COMMENT statement for the new table and columns.
Add COMMENT statements for new tables and columns in E05CMNT.sql.
Update E06GRANT.sql by adding a GRANT statement for the new table.
Update E07LOAD.sql by adding an INSERT statement for the test data.
Update ECOMDROP.jcl by adding a DROP TABLE statement.
Re-upload and re-run.
Upload all the modified DDL files.
Submit ECOMDROP and ECOMDDL.

div.p: There are common SQL and API error codes when configuring the ECOMAPP database. Solutions are provided for each issue.

h3.strong: ECOMDDL Fails with RC=12 - PLAN NOT FOUND

section.p: Cause: Db2 plan DSNTEP3 not available or no EXECUTE privilege.

h3.strong: ECOMDDL Fails with RC=12 - SQLCODE=-204 OBJECT NOT FOUND

section.p: Cause: DDL members not uploaded or incorrect dataset names.

pre.code:
1. Verify DDL members exist: TSO/ISPF 3.4 → <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.DDL
2. Check member names match exactly: E01STGDB, E02TBSPC, E03TABLE, E04INDEX, E05CMNT, E06GRANT E07INIT1, E07INIT2, E07INIT3
3. Verify SET HLQ statement in ECOMDDL.jcl matches your dataset name

h3.strong: ECOMDDL Fails with RC=8 - SQLCODE=-601 OBJECT ALREADY EXISTS

pre.code:
This is expected behavior when re-running ECOMDDL on existing database. Job will continue and create any missing objects.
To start fresh: 1. Submit ECOMDROP first 2. Then submit ECOMDDL

h3.strong: ECOMINIT Fails with RC=12 - Rest API failure with HTTP(S) status 500

section.p: Cause: SQL files have lines exceeding 72 characters.

pre.code:
All E07INIT files in zOS_Datasets have been pre-processed to comply with 72-character line limits. If you modified them:
1. Check for long lines: Edit member with ISPF COLS ON (show column numbers) Look for lines extending past column 72
2. Split long lines: - Break INSERT statements after table name - Continue column list on next line with leading spaces - Ensure VALUES clause on separate line
3. Re-upload corrected members

h3.strong: ECOMINIT Fails with SQLCODE=-803 DUPLICATE KEY

pre.code:
This is expected behavior with RC=8. Job continues to next step. Each step is conditioned on RC < 12.
To reload catalog from scratch: 1. Submit ECOMDROP first 2. Submit ECOMDDL 3. Submit ECOMINIT

section.p: Cause: Previous data not cleared, or partial load.

pre.code:
-- Check current countsSELECT'PRODUCT_VARIANTS'AS TABLE_NAME,COUNT(*)AS COUNT FROM<Your HLQ>.PRODUCT_VARIANTS;
Expected: 4,824If count doesn't match: 1. Submit ECOMDROP 2. Submit ECOMDDL 3. Submit ECOMINIT

Contact your Db2 DBA to grant CREATEDB authority:
GRANT CREATEDB ON SYSTEM TO <Your HLQ>;

section.p: Cause: Incorrect Db2 software library HLQ.
Cause: E01STGDB.sql uses default storage group.

pre.code:
1. Find available storage groups: SELECT * FROM SYSIBM.SYSSTOGROUP;
2. Update E01STGDB.sql: CREATE DATABASE ECOMDB01 STOGROUP <Your STOGROUP> BUFFERPOOL BP0 ...

section.p: Cause: Query not joining to STYLE_MODIFIERS table.

pre.code: -- Incorrect (missing PRICE_MULTIPLIER):SELECT PT.BASE_PRICE_CENTS FROM PRODUCT_VARIANTS PV INNERJOIN PRODUCT_TYPES PT ON...;-- Correct (includes multiplier):SELECT PT.BASE_PRICE_CENTS, SM.PRICE_MULTIPLIER, CAST(PT.BASE_PRICE_CENTS * SM.PRICE_MULTIPLIER ASINT)AS FINAL_PRICE FROM PRODUCT_VARIANTS PV INNERJOIN PRODUCT_TYPES PT ON...INNERJOIN STYLE_MODIFIERS SM ON PV.STYLE_CODE = SM.STYLE_CODE AND PV.MODIFIER_CODE = SM.MODIFIER_CODE;

section.p: Cause: Statistics not gathered or outdated.

pre.code:
1. Submit ECOMRS to gather/refresh statistics 2. Verify statistics exist: SELECT NAME, CARD, NPAGES FROM SYSIBM.SYSTABLES WHERE CREATOR = '<Your HLQ>';
If CARD = -1, statistics not gathered
